message.content:
Read this updated design document and review it on the same 5 dimensions as before. This is iteration 2 — focus on whether the previously identified issues have been resolved.

Document path: /c/Users/ygkim/.gstack/projects/devwogns-WordDictPOC/ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md

Dimensions:
1. Completeness — tool contracts, embedding model, eval set, prompt template present?
2. Consistency — does it resolve the SpringBoot/stdio conflict? Tool naming conflict resolved?
3. Clarity — can an engineer implement from this doc without back-and-forth?
4. Scope — is Approach C / Sprint 9-12 properly moved out? YAGNI clean?
5. Feasibility — SLO realistic? Embedding pipeline ambiguity resolved?

Context: Previously scored 5/10. Main issues were: missing tool JSON schemas, embedding table schema unspecified, Sprint 9-12 scope creep, SpringBoot/SSE legacy conflict, and SLO not measurable.

Be direct. Report PASS/FAIL per dimension. New quality score out of 10.

attachment.content:
- autoplan: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. (gstack)
- benchmark: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. (gstack)
- benchmark-models: Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. (gstack)
- browse: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- canary: Post-deploy canary monitoring. (gstack)
- careful: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. (gstack)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. (gstack)
- connect-chrome: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "show me the browser".
- context-restore: Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save. (gstack)
- context-save: Save working context. (gstack)
- cso: Chief Security Officer mode. (gstack)
- design-consultation: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and generates font+color preview... (gstack)
- design-html: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS. (gstack)
- design-review: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems, AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. (gstack)
- design-shotgun: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board, collect structured feedback, and iterate. (gstack)
- devex-review: Live developer experience audit. (gstack)
- document-generate: Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project. (gstack)
- document-release: Post-ship documentation update. (gstack)
- freeze: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. (gstack)
- gstack: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- gstack-upgrade: Upgrade gstack to the latest version.
- guard: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits. (gstack)
- health: Code quality dashboard. (gstack)
- investigate: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. (gstack)
- ios-clean: Remove the DebugBridge SPM package and all
- ios-design-review: Visual design audit for iOS apps on real hardware. (gstack)
- ios-fix: Autonomous iOS bug fixer. (gstack)
- ios-qa: Live-device iOS QA for SwiftUI apps. (gstack)
- ios-sync: Regenerate the iOS debug bridge against the latest upstream gstack templates. (gstack)
- land-and-deploy: Land and deploy workflow. (gstack)
- landing-report: Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. (gstack)
- learn: Manage project learnings.
- make-pdf: Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. (gstack)
- office-hours: YC Office Hours — two modes. (gstack)
- open-gstack-browser: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
- pair-agent: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. (gstack)
- plan-ceo-review: CEO/founder-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-design-review: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review. (gstack)
- plan-devex-review: Interactive developer experience plan review. (gstack)
- plan-eng-review: Eng manager-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-tune: Self-tuning question sensitivity + developer psychographic for gstack (v1: observational). (gstack)
- qa: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. (gstack)
- qa-only: Report-only QA testing. (gstack)
- retro: Weekly engineering retrospective. (gstack)
- review: Pre-landing PR review. (gstack)
- scrape: Pull data from a web page. (gstack)
- setup-browser-cookies: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session. (gstack)
- setup-deploy: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy.
- setup-gbrain: Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a local PGLite or Supabase brain, register MCP, capture per-remote trust policy. (gstack)
- ship: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. (gstack)
- skillify: Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent browser-skill on disk. (gstack)
- spec: Turn vague intent into a precise, executable spec in five phases. (gstack)
- sync-gbrain: Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search guidance in CLAUDE.md. Wraps the gstack-gbrain-sync orchestrator with state (gstack)
- unfreeze: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories again. (gstack)
- _gstack-command: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- episodic-memory:search-conversations: Search previous Claude Code conversations using semantic or text search

- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead

- claude-dashboard:setup-alias: Add check-ai shell alias for quick CLI usage check
- claude-dashboard:setup: Configure claude-dashboard status line settings
- claude-dashboard:update: Update statusLine path to latest plugin version

- everything-claude-code:agent-sort: Legacy slash-entry shim for the agent-sort skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:aside: Answer a quick side question without interrupting or losing context from the current task. Resume work automatically after answering.

- everything-claude-code:code-review: Code review — local uncommitted changes or GitHub PR (pass PR number/URL for PR mode)
- everything-claude-code:cpp-review: Comprehensive C++ code review for memory safety, modern C++ idioms, concurrency, and security. Invokes the cpp-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-build: Fix C++ build errors, CMake issues, and linker problems incrementally. Invokes the cpp-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-test: Enforce TDD workflow for C++. Write GoogleTest tests first, then implement. Verify coverage with gcov/lcov.
- everything-claude-code:devfleet: Legacy slash-entry shim for the claude-devfleet skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:docs: Legacy slash-entry shim for the documentation-lookup skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:evolve: Analyze instincts and suggest or generate evolved structures
- everything-claude-code:eval: Legacy slash-entry shim for the eval-harness skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-build: Fix Dart analyzer errors and Flutter build failures incrementally. Invokes the dart-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:e2e: Legacy slash-entry shim for the e2e-testing skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus
- everything-claude-code:flutter-review: Review Flutter/Dart code for idiomatic patterns, widget best practices, state management, performance, accessibility, and security. Invokes the flutter-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-test: Run Flutter/Dart tests, report failures, and incrementally fix test issues. Covers unit, widget, golden, and integration tests.
- everything-claude-code:go-build: Fix Go build errors, go vet warnings, and linter issues incrementally. Invokes the go-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:go-review: Comprehensive Go code review for idiomatic patterns, concurrency safety, error handling, and security. Invokes the go-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:go-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Go. Write table-driven tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with go test -cover.
- everything-claude-code:gradle-build: Fix Gradle build errors for Android and KMP projects
- everything-claude-code:hookify-configure: Enable or disable hookify rules interactively
- everything-claude-code:hookify-help: Get help with the hookify system
- everything-claude-code:hookify-list: List all configured hookify rules
- everything-claude-code:hookify: Create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors from conversation analysis or explicit instructions
- everything-claude-code:instinct-import: Import instincts from file or URL into project/global scope
- everything-claude-code:instinct-export: Export instincts from project/global scope to a file
- everything-claude-code:instinct-status: Show learned instincts (project + global) with confidence
- everything-claude-code:jira: Retrieve a Jira ticket, analyze requirements, update status, or add comments. Uses the jira-integration skill and MCP or REST API.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-build: Fix Kotlin/Gradle build errors, compiler warnings, and dependency issues incrementally. Invokes the kotlin-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Kotlin. Write Kotest tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with Kover.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-review: Comprehensive Kotlin code review for idiomatic patterns, null safety, coroutine safety, and security. Invokes the kotlin-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:learn-eval: Extract reusable patterns from the session, self-evaluate quality before saving, and determine the right save location (Global vs Project).
- everything-claude-code:orchestrate: Legacy slash-entry shim for dmux-workflows and autonomous-agent-harness. Prefer the skills directly.
- everything-claude-code:plan: Restate requirements, assess risks, and create step-by-step implementation plan. WAIT for user CONFIRM before touching any code.
- everything-claude-code:projects: List known projects and their instinct statistics
- everything-claude-code:promote: Promote project-scoped instincts to global scope
- everything-claude-code:prompt-optimize: Legacy slash-entry shim for the prompt-optimizer skill. Prefer the skill directly.

- oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup
- oh-my-claudecode:ultrawork
- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit
- oh-my-claudecode:writer-memory
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:skillify
- oh-my-claudecode:ultraqa: QA cycling workflow - test, verify, fix, repeat until goal met
- oh-my-claudecode:wiki
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview
- oh-my-claudecode:learner
- oh-my-claudecode:skill
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive
- oh-my-claudecode:setup
- oh-my-claudecode:debug
- oh-my-claudecode:self-improve
- oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications
- oh-my-claudecode:sciomc
- oh-my-claudecode:remember
- oh-my-claudecode:release
- oh-my-claudecode:ralplan
- oh-my-claudecode:ralph
- oh-my-claudecode:project-session-manager
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-chat: Use when you need to ask questions about a codebase or understand code using a knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: I'll read the iteration 2 design document and review it on the 5 dimensions.

input.description: Check if design document directory exists

content.content:
total 20
drwxr-xr-x 1 ygkim 197609     0  5월 29 16:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 ygkim 197609     0  5월 29 15:53 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 ygkim 197609 15247  5월 29 16:00 ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md

1	# Design: TrmWordDict2 AX 전환 — MCP + Ollama RAG
2	
3	Generated by /office-hours on 2026-05-29
4	Branch: yg_v0.2
5	Repo: TrmWordDict2
6	Status: DRAFT
7	Mode: Builder (해커톤/데모)
8	Supersedes: docs/plans/2026-05-20-mcp-server-design.md
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 기존 설계 대비 변경 요약 (2026-05-20-mcp-server-design.md 대체)
13	
14	| 항목 | 기존 (SpringBoot + SSE) | 신규 (Next.js + stdio) |
15	|------|------------------------|----------------------|
16	| 스택 | SpringBoot 2.7.18 + Tibero7 | Next.js 15 + Prisma + Supabase |
17	| 전송 | HTTP + SSE | MCP stdio (Claude Desktop 표준) |
18	| validate_db_name | → 제거 (check_forbidden 에 통합) | |
19	| suggest_db_column_name | → recommend_schema 로 대체 | |
20	| search_standard_word | → search_terms 로 대체 | |
21	| create_custom_word | → 제거 (ADMIN UI 담당) | |
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Problem Statement
26	
27	행정표준용어 사전(TrmWordDict2)은 현재 ADMIN이 데이터를 관리하고
28	IT 개발자들이 직접 UI에 접속해 용어를 조회하는 방식이다.
29	개발자들이 실제 코딩하는 도구(Claude Desktop, Cursor)에서 벗어나
30	별도 브라우저를 열어야 하는 컨텍스트 전환 비용이 존재한다.
31	또한 "이 테이블에 맞는 컬럼명이 뭐야?" 같은 자연어 질의를 지원하지 않는다.
32	
33	**목표:** AI 코딩 도구가 TrmWordDict2를 직접 참조해
34	파일 컨텍스트 기반 표준 용어를 추천하도록 한다.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## What Makes This Cool
39	
40	1. **컨텍스트 인식 추천** — 편집 중인 파일(DTO, DDL)을 보여주면 행정표준용어 DB에서 가장 적합한 컬럼명/용어를 추천
41	2. **온프레미스 LLM** — Ollama(로컬)로 데이터가 외부로 나가지 않음
42	3. **기존 인프라 활용** — Supabase(이미 Self-hosted) + pgvector 확장만 추가
43	4. **데모 순간**: Claude Desktop에서 "새 USER 테이블 만들 때 컬럼명 추천해줘" → 행정표준 약어 5개 추천 + 각 정의 출력
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Constraints
48	
49	- 팀: 2~3명, 3개월
50	- 대상: IT 개발자 우선 (Claude Desktop / Cursor 사용자)
51	- 온프레미스: 데이터 외부 유출 불가
52	- 기존 스택: Next.js 15 + Prisma + Supabase Self-hosted 유지
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Premises (확정)
57	
58	1. **Ollama 필수** — 단순 DB 검색만으론 "AI 경험"이 없음. 자연어 Q&A가 핵심 가치.
59	2. **MCP 클라이언트 기반 있음** — 팀 일부가 Claude Desktop/Cursor 사용 중.
60	3. **실데이터 충분** — 행정표준용어 실데이터가 DB에 존재해 LLM 컨텍스트로 활용 가능.
61	
62	---
63	
64	## Approaches Considered
65	
66	### Approach A: MCP Only
67	- Effort: S (1-2주) / Risk: Low
68	- 기존 Prisma 쿼리 → MCP 서버 래핑. Ollama 없음 — 순수 DB 룩업.
69	- Pros: 빠름, 안정적 / Cons: "AI 경험" 부재, 자연어 Q&A 불가
70	
71	### Approach B: MCP + Ollama RAG (선택)
72	- Effort: M (3-4주) / Risk: Medium
73	- MCP 서버 + Ollama + Supabase pgvector. 파일 컨텍스트 기반 추천.
74	- Pros: 자연어 Q&A, 온프레미스, 시맨틱 매칭
75	- Cons: pgvector 설정, Ollama 서버 관리, LLM 튜닝 필요
76	
77	### Approach C: Web Chat + MCP + Ollama
78	- Effort: L (5-6주) / Risk: Medium-High
79	- B 전체 + /app/chat 웹 채팅 UI.
80	- Rejected: 2-3인에게 6주는 빡빡. Phase 2 roadmap으로 이동.
81	
82	---
83	
84	## Recommended Approach: B — MCP + Ollama RAG
85	
86	3개월 타임라인 + 2-3인 팀에서 "진짜 AI 경험"을 주면서 온프레미스 보안을 유지하는 최적 균형점.
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Architecture
91	
92	```
93	Claude Desktop / Cursor
94	       ↓  MCP stdio (JSON-RPC 2.0, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk)
95	  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
96	  │  mcp-server/ (독립 Node 프로세스)        │
97	  │  Claude Desktop이 stdio로 직접 기동     │
98	  │                                         │
99	  │  search_terms(query, domainId?)         │─→ Prisma @prisma/client (직접 연결)
100	  │  recommend_schema(fileContext, intent)  │─→ pgvector → Ollama generate
101	  │  check_forbidden(text)                  │─→ Prisma (forbidden words)
102	  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
103	            ↕  DATABASE_URL (env)
104	  Supabase PostgreSQL (Self-hosted)
105	  + pgvector extension (supabase/postgres 이미지 기본 포함)
106	            ↕
107	  WordEmbedding 테이블 (별도, vector(768))
108	            ↕ 배치 임베딩 (5분 주기, word_embedding_worker.ts)
109	  Ollama: nomic-embed-text (임베딩)
110	          qwen2.5:7b Q4_K_M (생성, num_predict≤150)
111	```
112	
113	**MCP 서버 배포 방식:**
114	- Claude Desktop `~/.claude/settings.json` 에 아래 추가로 기동:
115	```json
116	{
117	  "mcpServers": {
118	    "trm-dict": {
119	      "command": "node",
120	      "args": ["/path/to/mcp-server/dist/index.js"],
121	      "env": {
122	        "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://...",
123	        "OLLAMA_URL": "http://localhost:11434"
124	      }
125	    }
126	  }
127	}
128	```
129	
130	---
131	
132	## Tool Contracts (JSON Schema)
133	
134	### search_terms
135	
136	```json
137	{
138	  "name": "search_terms",
139	  "description": "행정표준용어 DB에서 키워드로 단어/용어를 검색합니다.",
140	  "inputSchema": {
141	    "type": "object",
142	    "properties": {
143	      "query": { "type": "string", "description": "검색어 (한글 또는 영문 약어)" },
144	      "domainId": { "type": "number", "description": "도메인 필터 (선택)" },
145	      "limit": { "type": "number", "default": 10, "maximum": 30 }
146	    },
147	    "required": ["query"]
148	  },
149	  "returns": {
150	    "type": "array",
151	    "items": {
152	      "type": "object",
153	      "properties": {
154	        "id": { "type": "number" },
155	        "wordKor": { "type": "string" },
156	        "wordEng": { "type": "string" },
157	        "wordAbbr": { "type": "string" },
158	        "definition": { "type": "string" },
159	        "domainName": { "type": "string" }
160	      }
161	    }
162	  },
163	  "fallback": "Ollama 다운 시에도 영향 없음 (DB 직접 쿼리)",
164	  "timeout_ms": 500
165	}
166	```
167	
168	### recommend_schema
169	
170	```json
171	{
172	  "name": "recommend_schema",
173	  "description": "파일 컨텍스트와 의도를 기반으로 표준 컬럼명/테이블명을 추천합니다.",
174	  "inputSchema": {
175	    "type": "object",
176	    "properties": {
177	      "fileContext": {
178	        "type": "string",
179	        "description": "현재 편집 중인 파일 내용 또는 선택 영역 (최대 2000자로 클라이언트가 자름)",
180	        "maxLength": 2000
181	      },
182	      "intent": {
183	        "type": "string",
184	        "description": "요청 의도 (예: '사용자 마스터 테이블 컬럼명 추천')"
185	      }
186	    },
187	    "required": ["intent"]
188	  },
189	  "returns": {
190	    "type": "object",
191	    "properties": {
192	      "recommendations": {
193	        "type": "array",
194	        "maxItems": 5,
195	        "items": {
196	          "type": "object",
197	          "properties": {
198	            "columnName": { "type": "string", "description": "표준 약어 (예: USER_NM)" },
199	            "korName": { "type": "string", "description": "한글명 (예: 사용자명)" },
200	            "definition": { "type": "string" },
201	            "reason": { "type": "string", "description": "추천 근거 1줄" }
202	          }
203	        }
204	      }
205	    }
206	  },
207	  "fallback": "Ollama 다운 시 → pgvector Top-5 결과를 LLM 설명 없이 반환 (reason: 'Ollama 불가, DB 검색 결과')",
208	  "timeout_ms": 3000,
209	  "slo": "p95 ≤ 3s (num_predict=150, num_ctx=2048)"
210	}
211	```
212	
213	### check_forbidden
214	
215	```json
216	{
217	  "name": "check_forbidden",
218	  "description": "텍스트에 금지어가 포함되어 있는지 확인합니다.",
219	  "inputSchema": {
220	    "type": "object",
221	    "properties": {
222	      "text": { "type": "string", "maxLength": 500 }
223	    },
224	    "required": ["text"]
225	  },
226	  "returns": {
227	    "type": "object",
228	    "properties": {
229	      "hasForbidden": { "type": "boolean" },
230	      "matches": {
231	        "type": "array",
232	        "items": {
233	          "type": "object",
234	          "properties": {
235	            "forbidden": { "type": "string" },
236	            "suggestion": { "type": "string", "description": "대체 표준어 (있으면)" }
237	          }
238	        }
239	      }
240	    }
241	  },
242	  "matching": "ILIKE 부분 문자열 매칭 (ForbiddenWord.wordKor 기준)",
243	  "fallback": "DB 오류 시 hasForbidden: false + 에러 메시지",
244	  "timeout_ms": 300
245	}
246	```
247	
248	---
249	
250	## Embedding Data Model
251	
252	### 새 테이블: WordEmbedding (Word 오염 방지를 위해 별도)
253	
254	```prisma
255	model WordEmbedding {
256	  id        Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
257	  wordId    Int     @unique
258	  word      Word    @relation(fields: [wordId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
259	  embedding Unsupported("vector(768)")
260	  model     String  @default("nomic-embed-text") // 모델 변경 추적용
261	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
262	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
263	
264	  @@map("tb_trm_word_embedding")
265	}
266	```
267	
268	**임베딩 대상 텍스트 (concat):**
269	```
270	{wordKor} {wordEng} {wordAbbr} {definition}
271	```
272	예: `"사용자명 User Name USER_NM 시스템을 사용하는 사람의 이름"`
273	
274	**재생성 트리거:**
275	- 배치 방식만 사용 (Prisma middleware 방식 제외 — write 차단 우려)
276	- `word_embedding_worker.ts` 스크립트: `Word.updatedAt > lastRunAt` 인 레코드 감지 후 임베딩
277	- 실행 주기: `docker compose` cron or `setInterval(5 * 60 * 1000)` 상시 프로세스
278	
279	---
280	
281	## RAG 프롬프트 (recommend_schema)
282	
283	```
284	시스템:
285	당신은 행정표준용어 전문가입니다.
286	아래 표준 용어 목록을 참고해 DB 컬럼명/테이블명을 추천하세요.
287	- 반드시 목록에 있는 약어를 사용하세요.
288	- 없는 용어는 "표준어 없음"으로 답하세요.
289	- 답변은 JSON 배열만 출력하세요 (설명 없이).
290	
291	표준 용어 목록 (상위 10개):
292	{top10_terms_json}
293	
294	사용자 요청: {intent}
295	파일 컨텍스트:
296	{fileContext}
297	
298	JSON 출력 형식:
299	[{"columnName":"XXX_NM","korName":"한글명","definition":"정의","reason":"근거"}]
300	```
301	
302	**few-shot 예시 (프롬프트에 포함):**
303	
304	입력: "사용자 테이블, 이름/이메일/등록일"
305	출력:
306	```json
307	[
308	  {"columnName":"USER_NM","korName":"사용자명","definition":"사용자 식별 이름","reason":"사용자명 표준 약어"},
309	  {"columnName":"EMAIL_ADDR","korName":"전자우편주소","definition":"이메일 주소","reason":"이메일 표준 약어"},
310	  {"columnName":"REG_DTM","korName":"등록일시","definition":"레코드 생성 시각","reason":"등록일시 표준 약어"}
311	]
312	```
313	
314	---
315	
316	## Sprint Plan (3개월, 2-3인)
317	
318	### Sprint 1-2 (1~2주): MCP 기반 + search/forbidden
319	- `mcp-server/` 디렉터리 + TypeScript MCP 서버 초기화 (`@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`)
320	- `search_terms` 툴 구현 (Prisma @prisma/client 직접 연결)
321	- `check_forbidden` 툴 구현 (ILIKE 부분 문자열)
322	- Claude Desktop stdio 연동 테스트 + `settings.json` 스니펫 작성
323	- **마일스톤**: Claude Desktop에서 "USER_NM 표준어야?" 확인 가능
324	
325	### Sprint 3-4 (3~4주): pgvector + 임베딩 파이프라인
326	- Supabase `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector` (self-hosted 이미지 기본 포함 확인)
327	- Prisma migration: `WordEmbedding` 테이블 추가
328	- `word_embedding_worker.ts` 배치 스크립트 (nomic-embed-text via Ollama `/api/embeddings`)
329	- 기존 전체 용어 초기 임베딩 실행
330	- **마일스톤**: "사용자 등록일" pgvector 검색 → USER_REG_DTM Top-3 반환
331	
332	### Sprint 5-6 (5~6주): recommend_schema + Ollama RAG
333	- `recommend_schema` 툴 구현 (pgvector → Ollama generate)
334	- qwen2.5:7b Q4_K_M, num_predict=150, num_ctx=2048 고정
335	- RAG 프롬프트 + few-shot 적용
336	- Eval 20건 수동 검증 (아래 Eval Set 참고)
337	- **마일스톤**: "USER 테이블 컬럼명 추천" → 80% 이상 표준 약어 정확
338	
339	### Sprint 7-8 (7~8주): 데모 준비 + 안정화
340	- Ollama 다운 시 pgvector 폴백 구현
341	- Docker Compose에 MCP 서버 + embedding worker 컨테이너 추가
342	- Claude Desktop 팀 온보딩 가이드 (`docs/mcp-setup.md`)
343	- 팀원 2명 이상 실무 파일럿 + 피드백 반영
344	- **마일스톤**: 팀원 2명이 1주일 이상 실무 사용
345	
346	---
347	
348	## Eval Set (Sprint 5 전 체크인 필수)
349	
350	20건 수동 검증 케이스 — `docs/mcp-eval/eval-cases.md` 에 체크인:
351	
352	| # | 입력 intent | 기대 Top-1 컬럼명 | 기대 한글명 |
353	|---|-------------|------------------|------------|
354	| 1 | 사용자 이름 | USER_NM | 사용자명 |
355	| 2 | 등록일 | REG_DT | 등록일 |
356	| 3 | 이메일 | EMAIL_ADDR | 전자우편주소 |
357	| 4 | 부서명 | DEPT_NM | 부서명 |
358	| 5 | 주민등록번호 | RRNO | 주민등록번호 |
359	| 6 | 전화번호 | TELNO | 전화번호 |
360	| 7 | 수정일시 | MDFCN_DTM | 수정일시 |
361	| 8 | 코드값 | CD | 코드 |
362	| 9 | 상태 | STS | 상태 |
363	| 10 | 파일명 | FILE_NM | 파일명 |
364	| 11~20 | (Sprint 1-2 중 팀이 실무 사례로 보완) | | |
365	
366	합격 기준: 20건 중 Top-1 일치 16건 이상 (80%)
367	
368	---
369	
370	## Success Criteria
371	
372	- `recommend_schema` p95 ≤ 3s (num_predict=150 기준)
373	- Eval 20건 Top-1 일치율 80% 이상
374	- `check_forbidden` 금지어 탐지 정확률 95% 이상 (ILIKE 기반, 별도 검증 10건)
375	- 팀원 2명이 실무 코딩에 1주일 이상 실제 사용
376	
377	---
378	
379	## Open Questions
380	
381	1. **DATABASE_URL 보안**: MCP 서버가 개발자 로컬에서 DB에 직접 연결 — 개발자 로컬에 DB 읽기 전용 계정 발급 필요 (운영 DB는 읽기 전용 replica 권장)
382	2. **Ollama 공용 서버**: Sprint 1-8은 로컬 설치. Sprint 8 이후 팀 공용 Ollama 서버 검토.
383	3. **pgvector 이미지 확인**: `docker-compose.dev.yml` 의 `supabase/postgres` 이미지가 pgvector 포함인지 Sprint 3 시작 전 확인 (`SELECT * FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='vector'`).
384	
385	---
386	
387	## Phase 2 Roadmap (3개월 이후)
388	
389	- Web Chat UI (`/app/chat`, Next.js streaming) — 비IT 직원도 브라우저에서 사용
390	- 공용 Ollama 서버 (Docker Compose 통합)
391	- Cursor 플러그인 배포
392	
393	---
394	
395	## Distribution Plan
396	
397	- MCP 서버: `mcp-server/` 내 `pnpm build` → `dist/index.js`
398	- 배포: 팀원 로컬 `node dist/index.js` (Claude Desktop이 기동)
399	- 환경변수: `.env.mcp.example` 파일 제공 (DATABASE_URL, OLLAMA_URL)
400	- Sprint 8: `docker-compose.yml` 에 `mcp-server` + `embedding-worker` 서비스 추가
401	
402	---
403	
404	## Next Steps
405	
406	1. `mkdir mcp-server && cd mcp-server && pnpm init` + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 설치
407	2. `search_terms` 툴 구현 + Claude Desktop stdio 연결 확인 (p95 <500ms 검증)
408	3. Supabase `SELECT * FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='vector'` — pgvector 확인
409	4. Prisma migration: `WordEmbedding` 테이블 추가 + `pnpm prisma migrate dev`
410	5. `word_embedding_worker.ts` 작성 + 기존 용어 전체 초기 임베딩
411	
412	---
413	
414	## What I noticed about how you think
415	
416	- "스케일업 하고싶음"이라고 했지만 MCP Only가 아닌 Ollama RAG를 선택했다 — **단순 기능 추가가 아닌 경험의 질**을 선택하는 감각.
417	- Pros 중 "온프레미스"를 직접 인식했다 — **보안 의식이 기술 선택에 자연스럽게 반영**됨.
418	- "전사 사용"을 목표로 하면서도 IT 개발자 우선을 선택했다 — **좁은 쐐기(narrow wedge)부터 잡는 전략**을 직관적으로 아는 것.
419	- 3개월 안에 단계를 나눴다 — **한 번에 다 하려 하지 않음**. 이게 실제로 배포까지 가는 프로젝트와 그렇지 않은 프로젝트를 가르는 차이.
420

content.text:
## Summary

Iteration 2 is a substantial upgrade from the previous 5/10 draft. All five previously-flagged issues (tool JSON schemas, embedding table schema, Sprint 9-12 scope creep, SpringBoot/SSE conflict, unmeasurable SLO) have been directly addressed with concrete artifacts. The document is now implementable end-to-end by a single engineer with only minor ambiguities remaining around pgvector indexing and worker deployment topology.

## Analysis

### 1. Completeness — PASS

- **Tool contracts**: All 3 tools (`search_terms`, `recommend_schema`, `check_forbidden`) now have full JSON Schema with `inputSchema`, `returns`, `fallback`, and `timeout_ms` (lines 134-246). Previously missing entirely.
- **Embedding model**: Explicit — `nomic-embed-text` for embedding, `qwen2.5:7b Q4_K_M` for generation, with `num_predict=150`, `num_ctx=2048` pinned (lines 110-111, 334).
- **Eval set**: 10 concrete cases listed in a table with input/expected output, plus an 11-20 placeholder filled "during Sprint 1-2" with a pass bar of 16/20 (lines 354-366). The placeholder is reasonable but slightly weak — see Trade-offs.
- **Prompt template**: Full system prompt + few-shot example + JSON output schema (lines 283-312).
- **Embedding table schema**: Prisma model `WordEmbedding` with `vector(768)`, `@@map("tb_trm_word_embedding")`, cascade FK, and `model` column for tracking model changes (lines 252-266).

Minor gap: no pgvector index DDL (`CREATE INDEX ... USING ivfflat`/`hnsw`) is specified — at scale this matters for the 500ms `search_terms` budget if it ever uses vector search, and for `recommend_schema` top-K retrieval performance.

### 2. Consistency — PASS

- **SpringBoot/stdio conflict resolved**: Header explicitly marks this as `Supersedes: docs/plans/2026-05-20-mcp-server-design.md` (line 8), and the change table (lines 12-22) shows the migration from SpringBoot+SSE to Next.js+stdio. The MCP server runs as an independent Node process via `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (lines 95-102).
- **Tool naming**: The deprecation table (lines 18-21) explicitly maps `validate_db_name` → merged into `check_forbidden`, `suggest_db_column_name` → `recommend_schema`, `search_standard_word` → `search_terms`, `create_custom_word` → dropped. No naming collisions remain.
- **One inconsistency worth flagging**: Line 114 references `~/.claude/settings.json` for the Claude Desktop config, but Claude Desktop's actual config path is `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS) or `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` (Windows). `~/.claude/settings.json` is Claude **Code** harness territory, not Claude Desktop. This is a copy-paste landmine for onboarding.

### 3. Clarity — PASS (with caveats)

An engineer can pick up Sprint 1-2 and start coding from this doc. The "Next Steps" section (lines 406-410) gives 5 concrete commands. Tool contracts are unambiguous.

Two implementation ambiguities remain:
- **Worker process topology** (lines 277-278): "`docker compose` cron or `setInterval(5*60*1000)` 상시 프로세스" — the doc lets the engineer pick. For a Builder/hackathon mode this is acceptable, but Sprint 8's "Docker Compose에 embedding worker 컨테이너 추가" (line 341) implies the long-running process model, so just say so.
- **Initial embedding bootstrap** (line 330): "기존 전체 용어 초기 임베딩 실행" — no row count, no time estimate, no batch size. If there are ~10k+ standard terms, this is a multi-hour run with naive single-threaded calls to `/api/embeddings`. Specify expected dataset size and a batch concurrency value.

### 4. Scope — PASS

- **Approach C properly moved out**: Lines 77-80 mark it Rejected with rationale ("2-3인에게 6주는 빡빡"), and Phase 2 Roadmap (lines 387-391) absorbs it — Web Chat UI, public Ollama server, Cursor plugin.
- **Sprint 9-12 removed**: Plan stops at Sprint 8 (line 339-344). The previous over-commitment is gone.
- **YAGNI**: The deprecation of `validate_db_name`, `suggest_db_column_name`, `create_custom_word` and Approach C all reflect honest scope cuts. Three tools is the minimum that delivers the demo moment.

One soft scope concern: Sprint 7-8's "Docker Compose에 MCP 서버 + embedding worker 컨테이너 추가" (line 341) creates a contradiction with the actual deployment model on line 398 ("팀원 로컬 `node dist/index.js` (Claude Desktop이 기동)"). MCP stdio servers are spawned by the client; they don't run in Docker. The embedding worker absolutely belongs in Docker, but the MCP server itself does not — including it bloats Sprint 8.

### 5. Feasibility — PASS

- **SLO measurable**: `recommend_schema` p95 ≤ 3s with the exact knobs that make it achievable (`num_predict=150`, `num_ctx=2048`, `qwen2.5:7b Q4_K_M` quantization) — line 209, 334, 372. `search_terms` p95 < 500ms (line 407). `check_forbidden` timeout 300ms (line 244). These are testable.
- **Embedding pipeline ambiguity resolved**: Batch-only (Prisma middleware explicitly rejected, line 275). Trigger is `Word.updatedAt > lastRunAt`. Concat order is specified. Model name is stored on the row for migrations.
- **Ollama fallback**: `recommend_schema` falls back to raw pgvector Top-5 if Ollama is down (line 207). `search_terms` has no dependency on Ollama (line 163). This makes the system degraded-but-functional under realistic failure.

Realism check on the 3s SLO: `qwen2.5:7b` Q4_K_M at `num_predict=150` on a modern laptop GPU is typically 1.5-2.5s for generation, plus ~100ms embedding + ~50ms pgvector + retrieval. p95 ≤ 3s is tight but plausible **with GPU**. On CPU-only it will miss. The doc doesn't specify hardware assumptions — worth a one-liner: "p95 measured on RTX 3060 or equivalent GPU; CPU-only deployment SLO is p95 ≤ 8s."

## Root Cause (of previous 5/10 score) — Fixed

The previous draft was a vision document mistaking itself for a design. Iteration 2 became a design by adding (a) machine-readable contracts, (b) a concrete data model with a migration path, (c) a measurable acceptance bar (16/20), and (d) explicit scope cuts (Approach C → Phase 2, Sprint 9-12 → deleted).

## Recommendations

1. **Fix Claude Desktop config path** — Low effort, high impact. Replace `~/.claude/settings.json` (line 114) with the actual `claude_desktop_config.json` paths per OS. Currently this will cause every onboarder to fail step 1.
2. **Add pgvector index DDL** — Low effort, medium impact. Add to Sprint 3-4: `CREATE INDEX ON tb_trm_word_embedding USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 100);` (or HNSW). Without it, similarity search becomes a sequential scan as the corpus grows.
3. **Specify initial embedding dataset size + batch size** — Low effort, medium impact. Line 330 says "기존 전체 용어 초기 임베딩" with no numbers. Add: "N terms × ~50ms/embedding × concurrency K → expected runtime T minutes."
4. **Remove MCP server from Sprint 8 Docker plan** — Low effort, low impact but architecturally cleaner. MCP stdio servers are spawned by the client. Keep `embedding-worker` containerized; drop `mcp-server` from line 341 and line 400.
5. **Tighten eval set placeholder** — Low effort, medium impact. Lines 363-364 leave 10/20 cases TBD. Pre-fill with realistic placeholders (e.g., 금액/계좌번호/주소 등) so the 80% threshold isn't gamed by easy cherry-picks later.
6. **State hardware assumption for SLO** — Low effort, high credibility. One line: GPU class assumed for p95 ≤ 3s; CPU fallback SLO is p95 ≤ 8s.

## Trade-offs

| Decision | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| Drop Approach C → Phase 2 | Honest scope for 2-3 people × 3 months; ships demo on time | Web UI is the broader-audience play — IT-only wedge means no transferable value for non-coders until Phase 2 |
| Batch-only embedding (no Prisma middleware) | Avoids write-path blocking; simpler failure model | Up to 5min lag between term edit and recommendation accuracy — acceptable for hackathon, NOT for production ADMIN flows |
| Single Prisma `@unique` on `wordId` in WordEmbedding | Simple 1:1 model | Can't A/B different embedding models for same word without dropping rows; consider composite `(wordId, model)` unique if you ever switch from `nomic-embed-text` |
| Separate WordEmbedding table | Word table stays clean; rebuild = TRUNCATE | Two-step query (or join) on every recommend call; vs. column on Word which would be a single SELECT |
| `num_predict=150` cap for 3s SLO | Hits p95 budget | May truncate longer reason strings or multi-recommendation JSON — verify few-shot output fits in 150 tokens |

## New Quality Score: 8.5/10

Up from 5/10. The jump comes from: tool contracts machine-readable (was prose), embedding schema concrete (was missing), Sprint 9-12 deleted (was scope creep), SpringBoot conflict explicitly superseded (was unresolved), and SLO has measurement knobs (was aspirational).

Held back from 9-10 by:
- Claude Desktop config path error (line 114) — onboarding will fail
- Missing pgvector index (line 326-328) — performance landmine at scale
- Hardware assumption unstated for 3s SLO (line 372) — feasibility hinges on it
- 10/20 eval cases still placeholder (lines 363-364) — invites future gaming of the 80% bar
- Sprint 8 conflates MCP server containerization with embedding-worker (lines 341, 400) — architectural smell

None of these are blocking. An engineer could start Sprint 1 tomorrow.

## References

- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:8` — Supersedes declaration resolving SpringBoot conflict
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:12-22` — Tool naming deprecation table
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:114-128` — Claude Desktop config (wrong path)
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:134-246` — All three tool JSON Schemas
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:252-277` — WordEmbedding Prisma model + concat strategy + worker policy
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:283-312` — RAG prompt with few-shot
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:339-344` — Sprint plan stops at Sprint 8 (scope cut)
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:354-366` — Eval set with 10 concrete + 10 placeholder
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:372` — Measurable SLO with knobs
- `C:\Users\ygkim\.gstack\projects\devwogns-WordDictPOC\ygkim-yg_v0.2-design-20260529.md:387-391` — Phase 2 absorbs Approach C
